findings.evidence: The BulkImportAsync endpoint XML doc comment documents file format requirements but lacks documentation about the deliberate performance scope limit. The phase plan's Risks mitigation section states: 'Mitigation: Spec assumes class-sized rosters (20–50 rows). Add a comment documenting this assumption.' This documentation is absent.
findings.why_it_matters: Without explicitly documented assumptions about intended roster size, future developers may attempt to use this endpoint with large batch imports (1000+ rows), triggering the unmitigated risk: request timeouts and performance degradation due to synchronous processing. The deliberate scope limit becomes invisible to the next reader.
findings.required_action: Add a documentation comment (inline or in the XML doc summary) explaining that this endpoint is designed for class-sized rosters (typically 20–50 rows) and is not optimized for large batch imports; note that large files may cause request timeouts.

contract_sections_evaluated: Core: CORR (Correctness), DOM (Domain Integrity), OWN (Ownership & Boundaries), ERR (Error Handling), SEC (Security), RDB (Readability & Maintainability), CHG (Change Safety)
Context: TXN (Data Consistency & Transactions), CONC (Concurrency & Async), API (API & Compatibility), DEP (Dependency Hygiene), DOC (Documentation & Decision Trace)
Stack: DOTNET (Cancellation tokens, IDisposable, sync-over-async, DI lifetimes)
phase_constraints_verified: ✓ POST /users/bulk-import endpoint exists with lecturer/admin gate via RequireAuthorization policy
✓ Accepts file uploads in Excel (.xlsx/.xls) and CSV formats via [FromForm] IFormFile
✓ Parses header-name-mapped columns (Email, StudentCode, ClassName) case-insensitively using OrdinalIgnoreCase
✓ Returns 400 BadRequest if required columns missing; uses RosterFileParser error string
✓ Resolves ClassName → ClassId via ClassLecturerCache.FirstOrDefaultAsync with case-insensitive comparison
✓ Skips rows with 'unknown class' reason when ClassCache is null
✓ Resolves Email → User via db.Users.FirstOrDefaultAsync; skips with 'email not registered' if null
✓ Checks authorization per row using RosterAuthorization.AuthorizeAsync (Phase 4 helper); skips with 'not authorized for this student' if Denied
✓ Updates StudentCode only if non-null; always updates ClassId; marks rows as 'updated'
✓ Single SaveChangesAsync() call after all rows processed (atomic transaction)
✓ Returns 200 OK with RosterImportReport: totalRows, updatedCount, skippedCount, details array
✓ Details contain RowNumber, Email, Status ('updated' or 'skipped'), skip reason if skipped
✓ DB failure returns 500 Problem with message; no partial update
✓ XML doc comment documents file format: columns, case-insensitivity, StudentCode blank handling
✓ No replication/bypass of RosterAuthorization logic; correctly delegates to Phase 4 helper
convention_verification: ✓ RosterFileParser follows DocxTextExtractor pattern: static class, using var with isEditable: false
✓ BulkImportAsync follows RubricsEndpoints.UploadRubricAsync pattern: [FromForm] model, DisableAntiforgery(), try-catch DbUpdateException
✓ UsersEndpoints file is 209 lines, within norm (sibling RubricsEndpoints is 301 lines, both under 800-line limit)
✓ Response records (BulkImportForm, RosterImportRowResult, RosterImportReport) are sealed records/classes per C# conventions
✓ Column index math in GetColumnIndex (base-26 conversion) correctly handles A=0, Z=25, AA=26, etc.
✓ ParseExcelRow array sizing (maxColumn + 1) and Array.Fill correctly handles OpenXml's omitted blank cells
✓ CancellationToken propagated through all async calls: FirstOrDefaultAsync, AuthorizeAsync, SaveChangesAsync
✓ IFormFile.OpenReadStream() wrapped in await using for proper disposal
✓ SpreadsheetDocument.Open wrapped in using var for proper disposal
correctness_checks: ✓ Email normalization: Trim().ToLowerInvariant() before comparison and storage
✓ ClassName normalization: Trim() and case-insensitive DB comparison (ToLower() on both sides)
✓ Row numbering: RowNumber = i + 1 where i starts at 1 (first data row), so row 2 = first data (row 1 is header)
✓ StudentCode null handling: only updated if row.StudentCode is not null
✓ Authorization result checking: only skips if == Denied; proceeds for Admin/ClassLecturer/Grader
✓ Total rows count: uses parseResult.Rows.Count (all parsed rows)
✓ Row skip conditions follow phase Step 3 exact order: ClassName resolve → Email resolve → authorization
security_checks: ✓ SEC_PARAMETERIZED_QUERY: All queries use EF Core LINQ, not string concatenation
✓ SEC_SERVER_SIDE_AUTHZ: Authorization checked server-side via RosterAuthorization; no client-side bypass
✓ SEC_UPLOAD_CONSTRAINED: File type limited (.xlsx/.xls/.csv); size/row limit is deliberate design choice documented in phase Risks
error_handling: ✓ ERR_TAXONOMY_CONSISTENT: Parse errors → 400, row skips → 200 with reason, DB error → 500
✓ ERR_NO_INTERNAL_DETAIL_TO_CLIENT: Error messages are user-friendly, no stack traces
✓ ERR_CLEANUP_GUARANTEED: File stream closed via await using; DB transaction managed by EF Core
transaction_safety: ✓ TXN_BOUNDARY_MATCHES_OPERATION: Single SaveChangesAsync wraps entire bulk import operation
✓ TXN_NO_EXTERNAL_CALL_INSIDE_TXN: No external calls inside transaction
✓ Atomicity: All rows validated before any persist; single SaveChangesAsync at end; DB failure rolls back all
dependency_hygiene: ✓ DEP_JUSTIFIED_ADDITION: DocumentFormat.OpenXml 3.1.0 already used by Catalog service (verified in Catalog.Api.csproj line 18); no new vendor dependency introduced
